name: Chestnut Seaweed Rice
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup short grain brown rice (do not substitute)
1/4 cup sweet brown rice (a.k.a. glutinous brown rice)
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the chestnuts for at least eight hours, or overnight for a sweeter flavor. Do not discard the soaking water; organic chestnuts are preferred.
2. In a large bowl, soak the hijiki seaweed for at least one hour. Longer soaking time is unnecessary. (Arame can be substituted, but more will be needed as it doesn't expand.) Retain the soaking water and remove any sandy residue from the bottom of the bowl.
3. Mix the brown rice and sweet rice together and wash them several times until the water runs clear. Do not use other types of rice in this recipe, as pressure cooking would likely ruin their texture.
4. Combine the chestnut soaking water and hijiki soaking water with enough additional water to make 1 7/8 cups. Place this mixture in a pressure cooker along with the rice.
5. Cut the hijiki into pieces about one centimeter long and layer them on top of the rice in the pressure cooker.
6. Arrange the chestnuts symmetrically over the hijiki.
7. Pressure cook for 45 minutes. If the rice sticks to the bottom, let it sit for a few minutes before serving. The ideal result is chewy and cohesive; sticky rice is desirable in this dish.
Note: This recipe does not include instructions for using the pressure cooker or specific notes on cooking grains.
